multiRunConfidence.policy: Promote new blocking performance claims only after the baseline has at least five samples, the current trend has at least three samples, allocation budgets pass, and latency rows have an explicit ratio and rollback action.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when REST route allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the route hot-path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when path-parameter extraction allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the path-parameter change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when JSON binding allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the binding change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when middleware-chain allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the middleware change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when OpenAPI-disabled metadata allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the metadata change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when OpenAPI-enabled metadata allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the OpenAPI metadata change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when governance-disabled allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the governance path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when governance-enabled allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the governance rule-match change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

unsupportedSurfaces.requiredEvidence: Add a dedicated gateway proxy benchmark with five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budget, latency policy, and rollback guidance.
unsupportedSurfaces.adopterAction: Do not claim gateway proxy performance as ratcheted until dedicated benchmark evidence is published.

unsupportedSurfaces.requiredEvidence: Add a dedicated cache hot-path benchmark with five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budget, cache-mode notes, and rollback guidance.
unsupportedSurfaces.adopterAction: Do not claim cache hot-path performance as ratcheted until dedicated benchmark evidence is published.

adopterActions.trigger: Any tracked benchmark allocs/op median exceeds the ratchet tolerance under make bench-regression-check
adopterActions.action: Block the release, attach bench/regression-report.json, and revert or pin the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

adopterActions.trigger: Any report-only latency trend regresses before promotion criteria and current trend samples are attached
adopterActions.action: Keep the release decision non-blocking, investigate the hot path, and require three-sample trend evidence before promoting the row.

adopterActions.trigger: gateway or cache performance is claimed without a dedicated benchmark row
adopterActions.action: Downgrade the claim to report-only and require dedicated baseline, current trend, allocation budget, and rollback evidence.

adopterPerformanceContract.policy: Adopters may treat only ratcheted allocation budgets and explicitly promoted latency rows as blocking; RPC, gateway, cache, and unpromoted HTTP latency rows remain trend evidence until promotion criteria pass.

blockingSurfaces.adopterAction: Block release when allocs/op median regresses beyond the ratchet tolerance for the REST route hot path.
blockingSurfaces.rollbackAction: Revert the route hot-path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

blockingSurfaces.adopterAction: Block release when promoted governance rule-match latency or allocation budgets regress under bench-regression-check.
blockingSurfaces.rollbackAction: Disable the new governance hot-path change or keep the previous policy implementation until benchmark evidence recovers.

reportOnlySurfaces.adopterAction: Use unpromoted HTTP latency rows as trend evidence, not release blockers, until the ratchet promotes a row.
reportOnlySurfaces.rollbackAction: Investigate trend regressions before promotion and keep allocation budgets as the blocking guardrail.

reportOnlySurfaces.adopterAction: Treat RPC rows as candidate trend evidence and keep latency-critical methods on Kitex or existing gRPC paths.
reportOnlySurfaces.rollbackAction: Block RPC Tier 1 promotion until release trains and blocking budget evidence are attached.

unsupportedSurfaces.requiredEvidence: dedicated gateway proxy benchmark with five baseline samples, current trend samples, allocation budget, and rollback guidance
unsupportedSurfaces.adopterAction: Do not claim gateway proxy performance as ratcheted until a dedicated benchmark row is published.
unsupportedSurfaces.rollbackAction: Keep gateway performance claims report-only and rely on functional smoke gates for adoption.

unsupportedSurfaces.requiredEvidence: dedicated cache hot-path benchmark with five baseline samples, current trend samples, allocation budget, and cache-mode notes
unsupportedSurfaces.adopterAction: Do not claim cache hot-path performance as ratcheted until dedicated benchmark evidence exists.
unsupportedSurfaces.rollbackAction: Keep cache adoption decisions tied to behavior tests and observability until benchmark evidence is published.

promotionRules: latency rows promoted only after documented reason and maxRegressionRatio
rollback guidance remains attached for candidate RPC rows

promoted.reason: OpenAPI disabled route metadata has stable five-sample baseline evidence and current smoke is below baseline.
OpenAPI enabled route metadata has stable five-sample baseline evidence and current smoke is below baseline.
Governance disabled hot path has stable five-sample baseline evidence, no allocation drift, and current smoke is below baseline.
Governance enabled policy path has stable five-sample baseline evidence, no allocation drift, and current smoke is below baseline.

surfacePolicy.evidence: five-sample baseline is present and allocs/op median is blocking; latency stays report-only until current trend samples are promoted
five-sample baseline and current smoke are available for the enabled governance policy path

surfacePolicy.currentBlocker: RPC unary remains outside trackedBenchmarks until allocation and latency evidence satisfy the RPC promotion criteria
bench/ does not yet publish a dedicated gateway proxy baseline/current row
surfacePolicy.requiredEvidence: add a gateway proxy benchmark with at least five baseline samples, current trend samples, allocation budget, and rollback guidance before promotion

surfacePolicy.currentBlocker: cache examples and smoke gates exist, but bench/ does not yet publish a dedicated cache hot-path baseline/current row
surfacePolicy.requiredEvidence: add a cache hot-path benchmark with at least five baseline samples, current trend samples, allocation budget, and cache-mode notes before promotion

releasePromotion.rollbackGuidance: keep latency-critical methods on Kitex or existing gRPC paths until RPC candidates have blocking budget evidence

candidates.currentBlocker: latest smoke exceeded allocation baseline, so it cannot enter trackedBenchmarks yet
current scoped run may use mode-specific stream rows instead of the aggregate benchmark
mode-specific stream baseline is not yet published with five samples
mode-specific stream baseline is not yet published with five samples
mode-specific stream baseline is not yet published with five samples

promotionCriteria: latency median below baseline * proposed maxRegressionRatio for two consecutive releases
Kitex rollback note remains documented for latency-critical methods
